clauses.statement: Public Discovery selects exactly one target mode with base-url taking precedence over public-dir and repository source, and an invalid explicit input never silently falls back to another mode.

Built mode recursively scans up to 400 HTML files from an explicit repository-contained directory, rejects missing/non-directory/outside/symlink-escaped targets, and reads crawler evidence from that same root.

Live mode uses bounded read-only HTTP(S) GET requests for the base root and same-origin sitemap locations only, with manual redirect handling, a 40-page limit, a 2 MiB response limit, and a 10-second timeout.
scan_coverage records mode, roots, discovered_count, scanned_count, failed_count, errors, limits, status, and reason; zero scanned public pages is inconclusive and never a coverage pass.
Existing block and review findings remain stronger than coverage and preserve their fail or needs_review top-level result, finding IDs, severity, and suppression behavior.

The check pack forwards explicit Public Discovery inputs, implicitly includes Public Discovery for all when either target input is present, and emits a separate public_discovery.coverage row in JSON and Markdown while retaining inconclusive_count.
CLI help and the diagnosis package skill explain how to choose source, built, or live evidence and prohibit translating zero scanned targets or inconclusive into a clean deployed-site claim.

diagrams.title: Public Discovery target selection and verdict flow
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  I[CLI input] --> M{mode}
  M -->|base-url| L[live bounded GET]
  M -->|public-dir| B[built HTML walk]
  M -->|none| S[source convention scan]
  L --> C[scan_coverage]
  B --> C
  S --> C
  C --> F{findings}
  F -->|block/review| R[fail or needs_review]
  F -->|none and scanned > 0| P[pass]
  F -->|none and scanned = 0| X[inconclusive]

diagrams.title: Public Discovery filesystem and network trust boundaries
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  U[Untrusted CLI target] --> V{validate explicit target}
  V -->|public-dir| R[realpath repository containment]
  V -->|base-url| H[HTTP or HTTPS and same-origin policy]
  R --> W[bounded HTML walk max 400]
  H --> G[GET only manual redirect max 40]
  G --> Z[timeout 10s and stream limit 2 MiB]
  W --> A[scanner input]
  Z --> A
  V -->|invalid or escaped| E[inconclusive error evidence]
  G -->|redirect external non-HTML or fetch failure| E
  A --> O[findings plus scan_coverage]
